Edgar Xavier

Full Stack Software Engineer

About Me

I'm a full-stack software engineer with 6+ years building production systems across defense, civic, and commercial domains. I've led multi-year migrations, architected fault-tolerant systems for infrastructure-constrained environments, and shipped software that real people depend on.

Before writing software professionally, I spent 12 years in the US Army Signal Corps, deploying to Iraq and Kuwait to establish and maintain tactical communications networks. In 2017, I led a team to restore satellite communications for a field hospital set up in a coliseum after Hurricane María devastated Puerto Rico. That experience shaped how I think about reliability, accountability, and what it means to build something that cannot fail.

How I Build Software

I think about software as interconnected systems, not isolated features. Complexity compounds as applications grow, so I focus early on clean boundaries, honest contracts between components, and tests that reflect how a system actually behaves rather than just that the code ran.

I've worked in environments where the cost of a bad deployment is high: defense systems, compliance-driven platforms, hardware-integrated applications. That context has made me deliberate about architecture decisions and honest about tradeoffs.

How I Work With Teams

The best work I've done has been in small, close-knit teams where people know each other well, communicate directly, and take shared ownership of what they build. Most of my closest friends are people I've worked with. That's not a coincidence. I think the quality of the relationships on a team directly affects the quality of the software it ships.

My approach to leadership comes from the military: you lead by developing the people around you until they don't need you for that anymore. I've mentored developers from their first pull request to owning entire projects independently. Several have gone on to become mid and senior engineers. That outcome matters more to me than any individual feature I've shipped.

I also believe teams need to take care of themselves. Delivery matters, because without it there's no company. But a team that ignores its own health won't deliver for long. I try to be someone who notices that and says something.

Core Competencies

Systems & Backend

Node.jsExpressPostgreSQL
  • Multi-client API design with strict backward compatibility
  • GraphQL query optimization and overfetch reduction
  • Multi-tenant schema modeling with complex permission hierarchies
  • Offline/online state sync across distributed data layers

Frontend

VueReact
  • Legacy framework migration with zero functionality regression
  • High-frequency data stream throttling for real-time interfaces
  • Rendering performance optimization in large dataset components
  • Dynamic form systems with variable input types and PDF output

Infrastructure & Deployment

LinuxNginxAWS
  • Linux server provisioning and reverse proxy configuration
  • SSL termination, firewall management, and environment isolation
  • AWS S3 asset storage with least-privilege IAM policies
  • CI/CD pipeline setup and containerized deployments

Professional Experience

CACI — Full Stack Software Engineer

2021 – 2025

VueNode.jsExpress

Led a nearly two-year full rewrite of a deprecated Polymer frontend to Vue 3, maintaining 1:1 functional parity while the legacy system stayed in production.

Built two distinct Vue applications: a per-device capability dashboard and a server-side fleet management interface, enabling operators to monitor and remotely control up to 10 networked military communication devices.

Implemented UI-layer throttling to cap render updates at 1/second against a 100 updates/second data stream from OS-level sensors.

Established frontend test coverage from 0% to roughly 40-60% using Jest, introducing contract-based testing focused on component behavior rather than implementation details.

Borilabs LLC — Co-Founder & Lead Full Stack Developer

2019 – 2021

ReactAWS S3/IAM

Co-founded a software consultancy delivering production systems across five client domains over two years.

Built a fault-tolerant gate access control system integrating RF vehicle sensors and hardware controllers with a SQLite local database that synced to a central Node.js/GraphQL backend when connectivity was restored, designed specifically for Puerto Rico's unreliable power infrastructure.

Developed a special education assessment platform with dynamic test rendering and automated PDF report generation.

Built a nonprofit registry now hosting 1,169 verified organizations and tracking $439K in donations.

Resolved a React performance bottleneck in an SMS platform serving 50,000+ contacts by memoizing the contact list component.

From Code to Production

DigitalOceanLinuxNginxAWS

I manage my own Linux server environment on DigitalOcean, handling provisioning, deployment, and security configuration for personal applications. This includes configuring Nginx as a reverse proxy, managing SSL certificates, enforcing firewall rules, and maintaining system updates. I treat deployment as part of the engineering process, not an afterthought. Owning infrastructure decisions has strengthened my understanding of networking boundaries, runtime environments, and operational responsibility.

Open to software engineering opportunities, technical collaboration, and consulting conversations.
